title: Xolisma octandra
taxonomy: Xolisma octandra is a species of flowering plant in the family Loranthaceae, commonly known as mistletoes. The Loranthaceae family is a group of parasitic plants that obtain their nutrients by connecting to the vascular tissues of their host plants. Xolisma octandra, like other Loranthaceae members, is an obligate hemiparasite, meaning it requires a host plant to complete its life cycle. The exact classification of Xolisma octandra is within the order Santalales, which includes other parasitic plants. More research is needed to confirm the specific classification within the order.
